chunk_summary: This section focuses on nutrition support strategies for pediatric patients with acute severe ulcerative colitis, emphasizing growth preservation and evidence-based guidelines from ESPGHAN and NICE.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of nutrition in managing acute severe ulcerative colitis in pediatric patients.
Identify evidence-based guidelines for enteral and parenteral nutrition in pediatric IBD.
Recognize the importance of growth monitoring and nutritional interventions in pediatric ulcerative colitis.
key_facts: Acute severe ulcerative colitis in children often requires tailored nutritional support to prevent malnutrition and growth failure.
ESPGHAN recommends enteral nutrition as a primary strategy, with parenteral nutrition reserved for cases where enteral feeding is not feasible.
NICE guideline CG32 highlights the importance of individualized nutrition plans, including caloric and micronutrient adjustments.
Growth failure is a common complication in pediatric IBD, necessitating regular monitoring of height, weight, and BMI.
Nutritional interventions should aim to meet 120-150% of the recommended daily caloric intake for age and weight in malnourished children.
reference_quote: ESPGHAN recommends enteral nutrition as the preferred method for managing acute severe ulcerative colitis in pediatric patients, with parenteral nutrition as a secondary option when enteral feeding is not possible.
